code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test31-MAR-2019Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, and bilirubinuria, with low specific gravity, indicating possible renal tubular dysfunction and/or uncontrolled diabetes, though HbA1c is low. Cloudy, brown, ammoniacal urine may suggest urinary tract pathology, but no evidence of infection (negative nitrite/leukocytes). Recommend further nephrological and metabolic evaluation.

coding.display: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
